Off Calendar Dec-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS MATTHEW FEAVER, ROBIN SHEEHAN, SHEEHAN LAW GROUP PC, SHEEHAN & FEAVER PC's Motion For Leave To Amend Answer.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Sheehan, Sheehan Law Group P.C.'s and Sheehan & Feaver P.C.'s motion for leave to file a second amended answer is granted. The motion is off calendar as to defendant Matthew Jacob Feaver since he filed for bankruptcy. (See Notice of Stay of Proceedings filed 11/4/22; Freiberg v. City of Mission Viejo (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1484, 1488 ["a bankruptcy stay is only effective as to the party in bankruptcy; a plaintiff must proceed against nonbankrupt defendants."]; Shorr v. Kind (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 249, 258 [stay applies only to bankruptcy debtor].)

There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and this policy is especially strong with regard to answers. (See Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159 ["In particular, liberality should be displayed in allowing amendments to answers, for a defendant denied leave to amend is permanently deprived of a defense."].) While the motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324, there is no prejudice to plaintiff in granting this motion. A comparison between the proposed second amended answer and first amended answer makes it clear that defendants seek to plead more facts to support their defenses and clarify the causes of action that each defense applies to. In addition, the court would grant/sustain plaintiff's unopposed motion/demurrer with leave to amend anyway so there is no point denying the motion to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TOMI MENDEZ'S DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff's demurrer to defendant's answer is sustained with leave to amend.

It is common for answers to laundry-list affirmative defenses - common but not correct. Answers must aver facts "as carefully and with as much detail as the facts which constitute the cause of action and are alleged in the complaint." (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384; Edmon and Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 6:459; Opp. 8:18-20.) Here, the answer avers 16 affirmative defenses that are legal labels and little more. Other arguments need not be reached at this point.

Order is signed.

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-27-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROBIN SHEEHAN'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant Robin Sheehan's demurrers to the complaint are overruled.

First Cause: Fraud is pled with adequate specificity. Further, a complaint "may plead in the alternative;" factually and/or inconsistent allegations are permissible. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.)

Third Cause: "Common Counts, Money Had and Received" is pled, not "Common Counts for Goods and Services Rendered," as Sheehan states. (See CACI 370.)

Fifth Cause: While unjust enrichment is technically a remedy, it is often pled in California courts as a cause of action. The distinction is immaterial here.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
